data.narrative:
PlanQC is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alexander Glätzle, Sebastian Blatt, and Johannes Zeiher that has raised $50M+. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers (including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion).

Valuation analysis applied one applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using the $50M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $100M. A Peer-Median approach was not viable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-filter matches prevented reliable derivation—the comparison set lacked three or more companies at the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier.

The triangulated valuation range is $85M to $115M, with a median point of $100M. This range is supported by a single analytical method, which represents the primary constraint on confidence. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct stage-peer benchmarking data and dependence on the stage-progression multiplier, which may not reflect PlanQC's specific technology maturity, IP position, or market dynamics relative to the broader quantum computing landscape. Additional diligence on commercial traction and technical differentiation would strengthen conviction in any final valuation.
